summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their first regular meeting of 2022, conducting annual organizational business including electing Lisa Allen as chairperson and Bob Hauserman as vice chairperson. Clermont County Commissioner David Painter provided a state-of-the-county update covering Nestle Purina construction, road paving investments, and COVID-19 impacts. The board addressed multiple personnel actions in the fire department, approved the 2022 meeting schedule, set compensation rates for trustees, fiscal officer, and zoning board members, and discussed ongoing issues including the Tommy Combs property cleanup and upcoming zoning cases.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills from December 15, 2021 through January 11, 2022
Nominated Lisa Allen as Board of Trustees Chairperson for 2022
Nominated Bob Hauserman as Board of Trustees Vice Chairperson for 2022
Adopted the 2022 Board of Trustees meeting schedule
Scheduled 2022 July tax budget hearing for Tuesday, June 28, 2022 at 6 PM
Approved trustees' compensation at $2,025.33 per month from the general fund
Approved Fiscal Officer Laura Englert's compensation at $2,774.75 per month from the general fund
Set 2022 standard mileage rate at $0.585 per business mile
Set 2022 Zoning Commission member compensation at $50 per month, not to exceed $599 annually
Set 2022 Board of Zoning Appeals member compensation at $50 per month, not to exceed $599 annually
Approved sale of surplus equipment via govdeals.com (Resolution R-001-2022)
Approved 2022 road mileage for Goshen Township at 47.062 miles
Accepted $200 donation to Fire and EMS from Garnett Boyd
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Taylor Niemann effective 12-23-2021
Terminated probationary firefighter EMT Jared effective 12-28-2021
Tabled item Q regarding resignation of Sean McCarthy (staying on part-time)
Accepted two $25 Kroger gift cards from anonymous donor for fire department
Accepted $50 in Kroger gift cards from anonymous donor for police department
Approved training for Sergeant Pfeffelheimer to attend OACP Supervisor Training (STEP) at $2,150
Approved Sergeants Skates, Collier, Pfeffelheimer, and West to attend Supervising Patrol Critical Incidents Training at $294 per sergeant
Meeting adjourned at 7:53 PM
action_items: Chad to coordinate with truck manufacturer to get Goshen Township logo on new truck before OTA convention display
Chad to forward truck photos to board members when received
Harry Holbrook to continue monitoring zoning applications and notify board if July meeting is needed
Steve Pegram, Harry Holbrook, County Economic Development, EPA, and Attorney General's Office to continue weekly meetings on Tommy Combs property cleanup
Board members and department heads to confirm attendance at OTA conference January 26-28 in Columbus
Harry Holbrook and trustees to address solar panel exclusionary zoning requirements
Zoning Commission vacancies (two positions) need to be filled
key_amounts: $2,025.33 per month - Trustee compensation from general fund
$2,774.75 per month - Fiscal Officer compensation from general fund
$0.585 per mile - 2022 standard mileage reimbursement rate
$50 per month - Zoning Commission and BZA member compensation (max $599/year)
$200 donation to Fire and EMS from Garnett Boyd
$50 in Kroger gift cards donated to fire department
$50 in Kroger gift cards donated to police department
$2,150 tuition for Sergeant Pfeffelheimer OACP STEP training
$294 per sergeant for Supervising Patrol Critical Incidents Training
$4,000,000 - Clermont County commitment for road paving next year
$1,500,000 - Fines in Tommy Combs civil case
47.062 certified road miles for Goshen Township
1,600,000 square feet - Nestle Purina facility size
notable_topics: Annual organizational meeting - election of chair and vice chair
Clermont County Commissioner David Painter state-of-the-county update
Nestle Purina manufacturing plant construction progress
COVID-19 impacts on county employees and township staff
New fire chief Ed Myers sworn in (replacing Steve Pegram who became part-time administrator)
Tommy Combs property cleanup and bankruptcy proceedings
Upcoming zoning cases including Kerbett subdivision (117 units) and soccer facility on SR 28
New IT provider transition and technology upgrades needed
Traffic light approved at Smith Road and SR 28
New service department truck to be displayed at OTA convention
Road mileage certification including new Smith Road extension
Fire/EMS staffing changes and personnel actions
Death of Greg Carson, Williamsburg Township fiscal officer
Subdivisions and development growth concerns impacting emergency services
attendees: Ed Myers (new Fire Chief, sworn in earlier that evening)
David Painter (Clermont County Commissioner, Vice President)
notes: This was the annual organizational meeting for 2022. Steve Pegram transitioned from Fire Chief to part-time Township Administrator, with Ed Myers sworn in as the new Fire Chief earlier that evening. The meeting referenced the recent death of Greg Carson (Williamsburg Township Fiscal Officer, age 48) and illness of former Trustee Jim Allen (stroke related to COVID). Commissioner Painter mentioned DC vaccine mandate controversy and Brad Wenstrup's involvement. A 21-year-old died in a speeding accident on Woodville the prior Saturday night. The fire department conducted a training burn of a house at the park during the holiday break. There are two vacancies on the Zoning Commission. The govdeals.com resolution text mistakenly referenced 2023 instead of 2022.
title: Goshen BOT Regular Meeting 1.11.22
